libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment variables or system properties are required. Prefer in-memory/property-source based tests for core behavior; optional file-format modules such as YAML/HOCON/TOML/JSON are separate Hoplite artifacts and are not needed to reach meaningful hoplite-core coverage.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8367 [Automation] native-image run fails for com.sksamuel.hoplite:hoplite-core:3.0.0.RC1; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Hoplite 3.0 changes default environment-variable property-source behavior, so tests that use ConfigLoaderBuilder.default() may observe ambient CI environment unless they intentionally use an explicit source or defaultWithoutPropertySources/empty builder.
Config-file format coverage requires optional Hoplite parser modules and should not be added unless the target scope changes from hoplite-core to a format integration.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No concrete extra setup is required: hoplite-core is a standalone Kotlin configuration/decoding core whose Maven artifact brings its needed Kotlin reflection/coroutines dependencies transitively, and meaningful tests can exercise ConfigLoader, decoders, property sources, and preprocessors without Docker, external services, or additional format modules.
